description_long: This object is a small, upright stone statue depicting a standing human figure, likely inspired by ancient Egyptian statuary. The figure stands rigidly with arms at its sides, attached to a rectangular back pillar that extends from the base to the top of the head. The statue is mounted on a rectangular plinth, which is set on a textured, flat platform. The surface of the figure is smooth but shows some subtle color variations, with hints of reddish-brown on the arms and face, suggesting weathering or pigment remnants. The facial features are stylized and the body proportions are simplified, typical of ancient stone carvings. There are no visible inscriptions or decorative motifs on the statue or its base.
description: A small, upright stone statue of a standing human figure with a rectangular back pillar, mounted on a rectangular plinth. The surface is smooth with subtle color variations and no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the profile of the standing figure and the full height of the back pillar.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the face, chest, and symmetrical stance of the figure on the plinth.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other profile and the back pillar.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back pillar running from the base to the head, with the figure's back mostly obscured.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, rectangular stone plinth with a flat top and bottom, slightly wider than the figure. It is unadorned and merges seamlessly with the back pillar.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is the rounded head of the human figure, smooth and without any decorative features.
attribute_surrounding_features: The statue is placed on a flat, textured platform with measurement and color calibration strips, likely for documentation purposes.
